features.name: Plan Catalog (Solo/Team/Premium)
features.description: Canonical 3-tier SaaS plan catalog with included minutes, monthly/annual base prices, and per-minute overage. Prices are NET (tax_behavior=exclusive) with 19% German VAT added on top via automatic_tax. Annual = 10× monthly (2 months free). Catalog is idempotent via Stripe lookup_keys; price changes mint new prices and transfer keys.
risks: Live catalog must be re-created with live Stripe key at go-live; TEST catalog is currently active
19% VAT only applies if Stripe Tax registration is active in Dashboard

features.name: Safe Write Wrapper (stripe_call_safely)
features.description: Every mutating Stripe call goes through a centralized safe-write wrapper that: writes a billing_events row as 'pending' BEFORE the call, blocks writes to Connect-attributed subscriptions, enforces cross-org guard, carries idempotency keys, performs additive metadata merges, and verifies the result optionally.
risks: No auto-rollback on verification failure — detect-and-alert only
Idempotency window is 24 hours (Stripe server-side limit)

features.name: Per-Call Usage Metering
features.description: After each call is finalized via post-call webhook, usage (duration_seconds rounded to minutes via round()) is reported to Stripe's metered usage API. Idempotent via billing_usage_reports.call_id UNIQUE. All minutes reported regardless of quota — soft-stop model (no hard cap). Historical backfill explicitly excluded.
risks: Gated by STRIPE_USAGE_REPORTING_ENABLED flag — currently False by default
round() rounding means some minutes may not be billed (sub-30s calls round to 0)

features.name: Quota Warning & Over-Quota Notifications
features.description: After each call usage report, system checks if org has crossed 80% or 100% of included minutes. Sends one in-app notification + email per threshold per billing period (deduped via dedup_key). Over-quota is a soft-stop — agent stays online, overage billed automatically.
risks: billing_quota_minutes falls back to ai_minutes_quota if billing column null — dual-source risk
billing_period_start falls back to current month start if not set — may mis-count usage

features.name: Webhook Ingest & State Sync
features.description: Stripe webhooks are signature-verified on raw body, deduped by stripe_event_id UNIQUE, persisted, and processed in a background task. Signature failures are logged to billing_security_events and return 400. All other errors return 200 to prevent Stripe retries. Phase-1 handlers sync subscription state onto organizations table only — never write back to Stripe.
risks: current_period_start/end moved from subscription top-level to items in Stripe's 2025-03-31 API version — handled by _sub_period fallback
Failed handlers are recorded but not auto-retried in Phase 1

features.name: Checkout & Subscription Provisioning
features.description: Hosted Stripe Checkout session creates subscriptions with base + metered line items, optional trial period, automatic VAT, required billing address collection, tax_id collection for B2B reverse-charge, and promotion codes enabled. Self-service cancellation is DISABLED in the billing portal.
risks: DEFAULT_TRIAL_DAYS = 14 — needs Amber confirmation before go-live
Portal configuration cached per-process; cache lost on restart

features.name: Org-Customer Migration Matching
features.description: Dry-run matcher proposes CRM org ↔ Stripe customer links via email-exact or name-fuzzy (≥0.60 difflib ratio) matching with German legal suffix normalization. Proposals written to billing_migration_log only — zero Stripe writes until super-admin approves. Idempotent: orgs already in log are skipped.
risks: Name fuzzy matching at 0.60 threshold may produce false positives for similar company names
No email uniqueness guard — two orgs sharing an email could match the same Stripe customer

features.name: Super-Admin Billing Dashboard
features.description: Read-only cross-tenant view for super-admins: overview stats (total orgs, delinquent count, MRR estimate, YTD revenue), per-org billing state, Stripe account health, and migration match review queue. MRR excludes metered/overage items (flat base prices only); annual subscriptions normalized to monthly.
risks: MRR bounded at 1000 subscriptions, YTD at 5000 invoices — may undercount at scale
No RLS — requires require_super_admin dep for access control

features.name: Org Provisioning
features.description: New CRM org creation: creates auth user, organizations row, users row, agent_configs row, and seeds default required fields. Compensating rollback on failure. Deduplicates on heykiki_org_id and login_email. No billing objects created at provisioning time — Stripe customer created on-demand at checkout.
risks: Stripe customer creation is deferred — org can exist without billing link
Auth user deletion on rollback may fail silently

rules.name: Stripe Config Guard — Refuse All Calls Without API Key
rules.description: Every Stripe operation (read or write) is refused with StripeConfigError if STRIPE_SECRET_KEY is not set. The is_configured() check enables callers to gracefully degrade (return empty lists, skip usage reporting) without raising errors.
rules.purpose: Prevents accidental Stripe calls in dev/test environments without a key; makes billing module safely inert when unconfigured.
rules.trigger: Any call to get_stripe() or stripe_call_safely()
validations: if not settings.stripe_secret_key: raise StripeConfigError
rules.evidence: def _client(): if not settings.stripe_secret_key: raise StripeConfigError('STRIPE_SECRET_KEY is not set — the billing module is disabled.')

rules.name: Dual Feature Gates — STRIPE_BILLING_ENABLED and STRIPE_USAGE_REPORTING_ENABLED
rules.description: Two independent boolean flags gate billing functionality. STRIPE_BILLING_ENABLED enables the billing module overall. STRIPE_USAGE_REPORTING_ENABLED gates usage writes to Stripe (can be enabled independently once reads go live). STRIPE_USAGE_REPORTING_ENABLED requires STRIPE_BILLING_ENABLED — a config validation error is raised at startup if this dependency is violated.
rules.purpose: Allows reads (summary, invoices) to go live before usage writes; prevents half-configured live key scenarios.
rules.trigger: Startup config validation; per-request feature check
validations: STRIPE_USAGE_REPORTING_ENABLED=1 requires STRIPE_BILLING_ENABLED=1
sk_live key requires STRIPE_BILLING_ENABLED=1 and non-empty STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET
actions: Return config validation error list at startup
Skip usage reporting in post_call.py route if flag is False
systemEffects: Startup aborts in production if problems detected
failureConditions: Live key without STRIPE_BILLING_ENABLED or STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET configured
rules.evidence: stripe_usage_reporting_enabled: bool = Field(default=False, ...) ... if s.stripe_usage_reporting_enabled and not s.stripe_billing_enabled: problems.append(...)

rules.name: Pre-Write Audit Row — Every Mutation Recorded Before Execution
rules.description: Before any Stripe mutating call, a billing_events row with status='pending' is written to the database. After the call succeeds, the row is updated to 'succeeded'. On failure, updated to 'failed' with error_code and error_message. Even refused calls (Connect block, cross-org guard) are recorded. The audit row is NEVER skipped.
rules.purpose: Creates an immutable ledger of every billing action for reconciliation and debugging, analogous to agent_writes_audit.
systemEffects: billing_events row written before any Stripe call
failureConditions: DB write failure for audit row (propagates — audit must not be bypassed)
rules.evidence: # Audit row FIRST — every attempted mutation is recorded, including refusals. audit_id = _audit_insert(db, op=op, ..., status='pending')

rules.name: Connect-Attribution Write Block — Legacy ChatDash Subscriptions are Read-Only
rules.description: If a targeted Stripe subscription has a non-null 'application' field (indicating it was created by a Connect application, i.e., legacy ChatDash), all write operations are refused with ConnectAttributionError. Pure reads are still allowed. The subscription is flagged on organizations.billing_subscription_application when synced from webhooks.
rules.purpose: Prevents CRM from corrupting subscriptions created and owned by the legacy ChatDash Stripe Connect application.
validations: if application: raise ConnectAttributionError
actions: Update audit row to status='failed', error_code='connect_attribution'
rules.evidence: if application: raise ConnectAttributionError(f'Refusing write to subscription {subscription_id}: created by Connect application {application}. Legacy subscription is read-only in KikiCRM.')

rules.name: Cross-Org Write Guard — Subscription Must Belong to Org's Customer
rules.description: When a write targets a subscription, the system verifies the subscription's customer matches the org's stored stripe_customer_id. If they do not match, StripeCrossOrgError is raised. This is a defense-in-depth check supplementing JWT-based org isolation.
rules.purpose: Prevents a compromised org from writing to another org's Stripe subscription.
rules.trigger: stripe_call_safely() with subscription_id and org_id parameters
validations: org_customer != sub.customer → raise StripeCrossOrgError
actions: Update audit row to status='failed', error_code='cross_org'
rules.evidence: if org_customer and sub_customer and sub_customer != org_customer: raise StripeCrossOrgError(...)

rules.name: Idempotency Key Structure — op:org_id:sha256(payload)[:16]
rules.description: Every Stripe mutating call is issued with an idempotency key in the format '{op}:{org_id}:{sha256(payload)[:16]}'. The payload is JSON-serialized with sorted keys. Same op + org + logical input produces the same key, enabling Stripe to deduplicate retries for 24 hours server-side.
rules.purpose: Prevents double-charges or duplicate subscriptions on network retries.
actions: Pass as idempotency_key to Stripe API call
rules.evidence: def idempotency_key(op, org_id, payload): blob = json.dumps(payload or {}, sort_keys=True, default=str); digest = hashlib.sha256(blob.encode()).hexdigest()[:16]; return f'{op}:{org_id}:{digest}'

rules.name: Additive Metadata — Never Clobber Existing Stripe Metadata Keys
rules.description: When writing metadata to Stripe objects (e.g., adding heykiki_org_id to a Customer), existing metadata keys are always preserved. The merge policy is: existing dict merged with new values on top, but only non-None new values are applied. This prevents accidentally clearing keys set by other systems.
rules.purpose: Preserves any metadata already on legacy ChatDash customers; safe to re-run match approval.
validations: No-op short-circuit: if merged result equals existing, skip the Stripe call entirely
actions: Compute merged_metadata = {**existing, **{k:v for k,v in new.items() if v is not None}}
systemEffects: Audit row updated with status='succeeded', response_payload={'noop': True} on no-op
rules.evidence: def additive_metadata(existing, new): merged = dict(existing or {}); merged.update({k: v for k, v in (new or {}).items() if v is not None}); return merged

rules.name: Plan Catalog — Three Tiers with Graduated Metered Pricing
rules.description: Three SaaS plans: Kiki Solo (99 min, €99/mo, €1.00/min overage), Kiki Team (250 min, €179/mo, €0.75/min overage), Kiki Premium (750 min, €499/mo, €0.50/min overage). Each plan has a BASE product (flat subscription) and a METERED product (graduated: first N minutes at €0, then overage rate). Annual pricing = 10× monthly (2 months free).
rules.purpose: Soft-stop model: report ALL minutes to the metered item; included tier-1 minutes are €0; overage tier-2 bills automatically.
validations: interval must be 'month' or 'year'
Prices verified by lookup key drift detection (unit_amount, tax_behavior, tiers)
actions: Create/reuse Stripe Product for base and metered
Create/reuse lookup-keyed Prices with drift detection
Stale prices replaced by new price + key transfer
systemEffects: Stripe Products and Prices created/updated (TEST mode only until go-live)
failureConditions: Stripe API error during catalog creation
rules.evidence: PLANS = {'Kiki Solo': {'minutes': 99, 'monthly_cents': 9900, 'overage_cents': 100}, 'Kiki Team': {'minutes': 250, 'monthly_cents': 17900, 'overage_cents': 75}, 'Kiki Premium': {'minutes': 750, 'monthly_cents': 49900, 'overage_cents': 50}} ... ANNUAL_MONTHS = 10

rules.name: Tax Behavior — All Prices NET (Exclusive), 19% German VAT Added via Automatic Tax
rules.description: Every Stripe price (base and metered, monthly and annual, all tiers) has tax_behavior='exclusive'. This means displayed prices are NET; 19% German MwSt is added ON TOP at checkout via automatic_tax=enabled. Applies to base subscription and per-minute overage. EU B2B reverse-charge applies when a valid VAT-ID is collected at checkout.
rules.purpose: Amber's policy: VAT must never be absorbed into the plan price. Ensures correct German B2B invoicing.
preconditions: Stripe Tax registration must be active in Stripe Dashboard for 19% to be charged
validations: Drift detection checks tax_behavior == 'exclusive'; inclusive prices trigger new price creation
actions: Set automatic_tax={'enabled': True} on checkout sessions
Enable tax_id_collection at checkout for B2B reverse-charge
failureConditions: Stripe Tax not activated in Dashboard → no VAT charged despite automatic_tax=enabled
rules.evidence: tax_behavior='exclusive' ... automatic_tax={'enabled': True} ... 'TAX: every price is NET (tax_behavior="exclusive") — 19% German VAT (MwSt) is added ON TOP at checkout'

rules.name: Checkout Session — Required Billing Address + Tax ID Collection
rules.description: Stripe Checkout sessions always collect a full billing address (billing_address_collection='required') and enable tax_id collection. The customer's address is updated from checkout ('auto'). This is required for German invoicing (full address on invoice) and exact-rate VAT calculation. Promotion codes are always enabled.
rules.purpose: German invoicing law requires the full billing address. Exact VAT rate requires address, not just country inference.
preconditions: org has a Stripe customer (ensure_stripe_customer called first)
validations: interval must be 'month' or 'year'
catalog prices must exist for plan_title + interval
actions: Create Stripe Checkout Session in 'subscription' mode
failureConditions: No catalog prices found for plan/interval → StripeBillingError → HTTP 502
rules.evidence: billing_address_collection='required', tax_id_collection={'enabled': True}, allow_promotion_codes=True, automatic_tax={'enabled': True}

rules.name: Per-Call Usage Reporting — One Report Per Call, Rounded Minutes, No Cap
rules.description: After each call is finalized (post-call webhook, status='processed'), call duration_seconds is converted to minutes via round() and reported to Stripe's SubscriptionItem usage record API. The billing_usage_reports.call_id UNIQUE constraint ensures exactly one report per call even on webhook retries. ALL minutes are reported (no quota cap) — overage is a Stripe-level concept.
rules.purpose: Enables automatic overage billing; prevents double-billing on retries; keeps displayed usage (round()) consistent with billed usage.
preconditions: org has active metered subscription item
validations: Unique constraint on billing_usage_reports.call_id prevents double-billing
minutes > 0 required (zero-minute calls skipped with skip_reason='zero_minutes')
failureConditions: no_customer: org has no stripe_customer_id
no_metered_sub: no active metered subscription item
legacy_connect_sub: subscription is Connect-attributed
zero_minutes: call duration rounds to 0
rules.evidence: def minutes_from_seconds(seconds): return round((seconds or 0) / 60) ... call_id UNIQUE not null references calls ... action='increment'

rules.name: Historical Backfill Excluded from Billing
rules.description: Usage reporting is triggered only from the POST /api/elevenlabs/post-call route, not from the shared _process_one function used by history_import. This structurally ensures that backfilling historical ElevenLabs conversations into a new org's call log never generates Stripe usage records or charges.
rules.purpose: Prevents mis-billing new orgs for historical calls that predate their Kiki subscription.
rules.trigger: import_agent_history background task (explicitly NOT triggered from here)
actions: No billing action on historical import
rules.evidence: # Invoked from the post-call ROUTE (not the shared _process_one), so historical backfill via history_import never reaches this code → no mis-billing.

rules.name: 80% Quota Warning and Over-Quota Notification — Once Per Billing Period
rules.description: After each usage report, the system checks the org's cumulative call minutes since billing_period_start against billing_quota_minutes. If usage >= 80% of quota, one 'quota_warning' notification is sent (deduped per period). If usage > 100%, one 'over_quota' notification is sent (deduped per period). Both send an in-app notification and attempt to dispatch an email via the existing send_email() chain.
rules.purpose: Warns customers before overage costs begin; alerts them when overage is being incurred.
rules.trigger: report_call_usage() success (best-effort, never blocks billing flow)
preconditions: dedup_key does not already exist in billing_notifications
validations: Dedup via billing_notifications.dedup_key UNIQUE index prevents duplicate warnings
actions: If used >= 80% of quota: record quota_warning notification
If used > quota: record over_quota notification
failureConditions: quota=0: notification skipped
email failure: logged, in-app notification still recorded
rules.evidence: QUOTA_WARNING_PCT = 0.8 ... if used > quota: notify_over_quota(...) elif used >= quota * QUOTA_WARNING_PCT: notify_quota_warning(...)

rules.name: Notification Deduplication — One Alert Per Type Per Billing Period
rules.description: Billing notifications use a dedup_key (e.g., 'over_quota:{org_id}:{period_key}') enforced by a UNIQUE index on billing_notifications.dedup_key. A second notification of the same type in the same period silently no-ops (exception caught and ignored). This prevents duplicate emails on repeated usage reports in the same period.
rules.purpose: Prevents notification spam — customers receive at most one 80% warning and one over-quota alert per billing period.
validations: UNIQUE constraint on billing_notifications.dedup_key where dedup_key is not null
rules.evidence: create unique index billing_notifications_dedup_idx on billing_notifications (dedup_key) where dedup_key is not null; ... dedup_key=f'over_quota:{org_id}:{period_key}'

rules.name: Webhook Signature Verification — Raw Body Required; Failures Logged to Security Table
rules.description: Stripe webhooks are verified using stripe.Webhook.construct_event() on the raw request body (JSON must NOT be parsed first). Signature failures raise SignatureVerificationError → HTTP 400 → Stripe does NOT retry. Valid webhooks that fail processing still return 200 (so Stripe does not retry a processing bug). Signature failures are logged to billing_security_events with source IP and body excerpt.
rules.purpose: Prevents forged webhook injection; returns 400 only for authentication failures so retries are reserved for legitimate transient errors.
validations: stripe.Webhook.construct_event() verifies HMAC signature
actions: On success: deduplicate by stripe_event_id; INSERT billing_webhook_events; add background task
failureConditions: Missing or invalid STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET → all webhooks fail verification
rules.evidence: raw = await request.body() ... event = stripe.Webhook.construct_event(raw_body, sig_header, settings.stripe_webhook_secret) ... _record_security_event(...)

rules.name: Webhook Deduplication — stripe_event_id UNIQUE Prevents Double-Processing
rules.description: Each incoming Stripe webhook event is deduplicated by its stripe_event_id (UNIQUE constraint on billing_webhook_events). If the event_id already exists in the table, the endpoint returns immediately with {new: False} and no background task is enqueued. This handles Stripe's built-in retry mechanism transparently.
rules.purpose: Ensures each webhook handler runs exactly once, even when Stripe retries the same event.
validations: SELECT from billing_webhook_events WHERE stripe_event_id = ?
Concurrent INSERT race handled by unique violation exception catch
actions: If new: INSERT + enqueue background task
rules.evidence: billing_webhook_events.stripe_event_id text unique not null ... if existing: return {'new': False, ...}

rules.name: Webhook State Sync — Subscription Events Update Organizations Table (Never Write to Stripe)
rules.description: Phase-1 webhook handlers are pure inbound state-syncs: they read the Stripe event payload and update organizations.billing_* columns only. They NEVER write back to Stripe. Events for unlinked customers (no matching stripe_customer_id in organizations) are silently no-ops. Handled events: customer.subscription.created/updated/trial_will_end/paused/resumed/deleted, invoice.paid/payment_succeeded/payment_failed, checkout.session.completed.
rules.purpose: Keeps CRM billing state (plan, quota, period, status) in sync with Stripe as the source of truth.
actions: customer.subscription.*: UPDATE organizations SET billing_subscription_id, billing_status, billing_plan_title, billing_quota_minutes, billing_period_start/end
invoice.payment_failed: UPDATE organizations SET billing_status='past_due'; notify_payment_failed()
failureConditions: Handler exception: status='failed', notes recorded; no retry in Phase 1
rules.evidence: _HANDLERS = {'customer.subscription.created': _handle_subscription, ..., 'invoice.payment_failed': _handle_invoice_failed, 'checkout.session.completed': _handle_checkout_completed}

rules.name: Plan Derivation from Webhook — Plan Title and Quota Read from Product Metadata
rules.description: When processing subscription events, the plan title and included minutes are derived from the non-metered subscription item's product metadata fields 'plan_title' and 'included_call_minutes'. If the product is not expanded in the webhook payload, it is retrieved on-demand from Stripe. If derivation fails, the subscription status still syncs but plan/quota are not updated.
rules.purpose: Ties the org's quota minutes to the Stripe product definition — quota is the source of truth for billing warnings.
validations: Skips metered (usage_type='metered') items — only reads base product
included_call_minutes parsed with int(float()) to handle string values
actions: For non-metered item: retrieve product metadata
failureConditions: Product retrieval fails: exception caught, meta={}, quota not updated
rules.evidence: meta.get('plan_title') ... int(float(meta.get('included_call_minutes'))) ... if recurring.get('usage_type') == 'metered': continue

rules.name: Subscription Period Sync — Dual-Location Field Read for API Compatibility
rules.description: current_period_start and current_period_end are read from the subscription's top-level fields first, then fall back to the first subscription item if top-level values are absent. This accommodates Stripe's 2025-03-31 API version change that moved these fields from the subscription object to subscription items.
rules.purpose: Ensures period-based usage tracking and notifications work correctly across Stripe API versions.
actions: Read top-level, fallback to first item if None
rules.evidence: # The 2025-03-31.basil API moved current_period_* OFF the subscription object ONTO its items — webhook payloads now only carry them on items.data[0]. Read top-level first (older versions / SDK back-fill), fall back to the first item.

rules.name: Billing Summary — Used Minutes Calculation Mirrors Billed Minutes
rules.description: The billing summary shown to customers (GET /api/billing/summary) calculates used_minutes as round(sum(duration_seconds) / 60) for all calls since billing_period_start. This exactly mirrors the minutes_from_seconds() function used in usage reporting, so displayed usage always matches the billed amount. period_start defaults to the 1st of the current month if not set.
rules.purpose: Prevents drift between the displayed usage in the UI and the billed amount in Stripe.
actions: Sum all call duration_seconds since billing_period_start
outputs: BillingSummary with quota_minutes, used_minutes, used_percent, over_quota
rules.evidence: return round(sum((c.get('duration_seconds') or 0) for c in calls) / 60) ... # Mirrors settings._usage exactly (round(sum(duration_seconds)/60)) so the 'minutes used' shown in Abrechnung matches the rest of the app.

rules.name: Billing Portal — Self-Service Cancellation Disabled; Custom Configuration Required
rules.description: The Stripe Customer Portal is configured with a custom Configuration object that explicitly disables subscription_cancel. This prevents customers from cancelling their subscription via the portal. Customers can update address, email, phone, tax_id, and payment methods, and can view invoice history. The custom config also prevents the 'default configuration not created' 502 error.
rules.purpose: Amber's policy: cancellations are handled only via email or phone (info@kikichat.de), not self-service.
actions: Create billing_portal.Session with the custom config
systemEffects: Stripe billing portal Configuration created (cached per-process)
failureConditions: Stripe API error → HTTP 502 with German error message
Config creation failure: falls back to default config session
rules.evidence: 'subscription_cancel': {'enabled': False} ... # Amber's policy: NO self-service cancellation in the portal.

rules.name: Stripe Customer Creation — Idempotent, With Org Address and heykiki_org_id Metadata
rules.description: When starting a checkout session, the system ensures the org has a Stripe customer (ensure_stripe_customer). If stripe_customer_id already exists on the org, the existing customer is returned. If not, a new Customer is created with the org's name, email, address (German address keys tolerated), preferred_locales=['de'], and metadata with heykiki_org_id and org_id. The org is then updated with the customer_id.
rules.purpose: Creates a one-to-one mapping between CRM org and Stripe customer; enables address-based exact VAT calculation.
validations: idempotency_payload={'org_id': str(org_id)} prevents duplicate customer creation
actions: Create Stripe Customer with org metadata
Map German address fields to Stripe address
failureConditions: org not found → StripeBillingError
rules.evidence: if org.get('stripe_customer_id'): return org['stripe_customer_id'] ... 'preferred_locales': ['de'], 'metadata': {'heykiki_org_id': ..., 'org_id': str(org_id)}

rules.name: Org-Customer Matching — Email-Exact (95% confidence) and Name-Fuzzy (≥60% ratio) Matching
rules.description: The dry-run matcher scans unlinked orgs (no stripe_customer_id) and proposes a Stripe customer match via: (1) email_exact: org.email == customer.email, confidence=0.95; (2) name_fuzzy: German legal suffix-stripped difflib.SequenceMatcher ratio ≥ 0.60, confidence=ratio. Orgs with any existing billing_migration_log row are skipped (idempotent). Proposals are written to billing_migration_log with status='proposed'.
rules.purpose: Bridges the legacy ChatDash Stripe account to the CRM without requiring manual data entry for ~80 existing customers.
validations: Skip org if billing_migration_log already has a row for it
name_fuzzy threshold: ratio >= 0.60
Longest German legal suffix stripped first to improve name comparison
actions: For each unlinked org: find best email match, then best name match
INSERT billing_migration_log with method, confidence, candidate snapshot
rules.evidence: _NAME_THRESHOLD = 0.60 ... if org_email and org_email in by_email: method, confidence, candidate = 'email_exact', 0.95, by_email[org_email]

rules.name: Match Approval — Writes heykiki_org_id to Stripe Customer Metadata + Links Org
rules.description: Super-admin approval of a proposed match writes heykiki_org_id and org_id into the Stripe customer's metadata (additive merge) and updates organizations.stripe_customer_id. Cross-org guard: refuses if the customer_id is already linked to a different org. Match must be in status='proposed' to be approved.
rules.purpose: Phase-2 write-back that completes the org-customer link; enables subscription sync and usage reporting for pre-existing customers.
preconditions: customer not already linked to another org
validations: match must be in 'proposed' status
stripe_customer_id must be present
Cross-org guard: customer_id not linked to another org
actions: UPDATE billing_migration_log SET status='approved', reviewed_by, reviewed_at
failureConditions: Match not found → StripeBillingError
Match not in 'proposed' status → StripeBillingError
Customer already linked to another org → StripeBillingError → HTTP 400
rules.evidence: if other: raise StripeBillingError(f'customer {customer_id} is already linked to another org') ... stripe_call_safely(op='customer.metadata_writeback', metadata_merge={'heykiki_org_id': ..., 'org_id': ...})

rules.name: Subscription Cancellation — Cancel at Period End, Refused on Legacy Connect Subs
rules.description: Super-admin can cancel a subscription by setting cancel_at_period_end=True on Stripe. The cancel is refused with ConnectAttributionError (HTTP 409) if the subscription is a legacy ChatDash Connect subscription. The org's billing_subscription_id is used to target the subscription.
rules.purpose: Allows HeyKiki operators to cancel subscriptions while protecting legacy ChatDash customers from accidental modification.
validations: BILL-004 Connect block applies — raises ConnectAttributionError → HTTP 409
systemEffects: Stripe subscription flagged to cancel at period end
failureConditions: No billing_subscription_id → StripeBillingError → HTTP 400
Connect-attributed sub → ConnectAttributionError → HTTP 409
rules.evidence: result = stripe_call_safely(op='subscription.cancel_at_period_end', subscription_id=sub_id, builder=lambda ...: Subscription.modify(sub_id, cancel_at_period_end=True, ...))

rules.name: Retry Open Invoice — First Open Invoice Only, Idempotent by Invoice ID
rules.description: Super-admin can trigger a payment retry on the org's most recent open invoice. The system fetches the first open invoice for the Stripe customer (limit=1) and calls Invoice.pay(). Idempotent by invoice_id. Returns no_open_invoice if no open invoice exists.
rules.purpose: Allows dunning/recovery from payment failures without requiring customer action.
preconditions: org has at least one open invoice
failureConditions: No stripe_customer_id → StripeBillingError → HTTP 400
rules.evidence: open_invoices = get_stripe().Invoice.list(customer=customer_id, status='open', limit=1).data ... Invoice.pay(invoice_id, idempotency_key=idem)

rules.name: Billing Sync Endpoint — Webhook Fallback for Post-Checkout State Sync
rules.description: POST /api/billing/sync allows the frontend to pull the org's current Stripe subscription state immediately after a checkout redirect (when webhooks may not have arrived yet in dev or before webhook setup). It reads all subscriptions, picks the primary one (prefers active/trialing/past_due/unpaid over canceled/incomplete by recency), and syncs via the same handler as webhooks. Idempotent (no Stripe writes).
rules.purpose: Provides immediate post-checkout UI refresh without waiting for webhook delivery; safe to call at any time.
actions: List all subscriptions for customer (status='all', limit=10)
Pick primary by live status preference + created desc
rules.evidence: _LIVE_SUB_STATES = {'active', 'trialing', 'past_due', 'unpaid'} ... sub = _pick_primary_subscription((result or {}).get('data') or []) ... _handle_subscription(client, sub)

rules.name: RLS Stance — All Billing Tables Backend-Only (Service Role Bypasses, No Client Policy = Deny-All)
rules.description: All 7 billing tables (billing_events, billing_webhook_events, billing_usage_reports, billing_migration_log, billing_security_events, billing_notifications, billing_checkout_sessions) have RLS enabled with no client-facing policy. This means any browser JWT request is denied. Only the service role (backend) can read or write these tables.
rules.purpose: Prevents customers or admins from reading/writing billing audit data via the client SDK; keeps billing strictly server-side.
rules.trigger: Database access via client JWT token
validations: RLS enabled on all billing tables
No RLS policies created → deny-all for non-service-role
rules.evidence: alter table billing_events enable row level security; ... -- RLS (backend-only tables; service role bypasses; NO client policy) -- enabled + no policy ⇒ never readable by the browser.

rules.name: Trial Period — 14 Days Default, Set on Checkout Session
rules.description: New subscriptions created via checkout may include a trial period (trial_period_days). The default is DEFAULT_TRIAL_DAYS=14. Trial days are passed as subscription_data.trial_period_days in the Checkout Session. trial_will_end webhook (fired 3 days before trial expiry) triggers notify_trial_will_end() which sends an in-app + email notification.
rules.purpose: Allows prospects to evaluate the product before being charged.
systemEffects: Stripe subscription starts in trialing status
rules.evidence: DEFAULT_TRIAL_DAYS = 14  # ⚠️ confirm with Amber before go-live ... if trial_days and trial_days > 0: sub_data['trial_period_days'] = trial_days

rules.name: Org Provisioning — No Billing Objects at Provisioning Time
rules.description: Provisioning a new org (POST /api/heykiki/provision) creates organizations, users, agent_configs, and agent_required_fields rows but does NOT create a Stripe Customer or subscription. Billing is decoupled from provisioning — the Stripe customer is created on-demand when the org initiates a checkout. This allows orgs to be provisioned before billing is configured.
rules.purpose: Decouples onboarding from billing; allows new orgs to exist in the system before subscribing.
inputs: ProvisionRequest: heykiki_org_id, org_name, login_email, login_password, admin_name, contact_email, elevenlabs_agent_id
validations: Rejects duplicate heykiki_org_id (HTTP 409)
Rejects duplicate login_email (HTTP 409)
actions: Insert organizations row (no billing columns set)
systemEffects: organizations, users, agent_configs, agent_required_fields rows created
failureConditions: Duplicate heykiki_org_id → HTTP 409
Duplicate login_email → HTTP 409
Auth user creation failure → HTTP 502
Any subsequent failure → compensating rollback
rules.evidence: # P0.9 — Fresh-tenant audit: this function inserts the three DB rows listed below and configures the EL agent. NO ... billing ... seeded.

rules.name: MRR Estimation — Flat Base Prices Only, Annual Normalized to Monthly
rules.description: Super-admin billing overview estimates MRR by summing all active subscription items' unit_amount × quantity, skipping metered (usage_type='metered') items. Annual prices are divided by 12 to normalize to monthly. Bounded at 1000 subscriptions. YTD revenue sums all paid invoices since Jan 1 of the current year, bounded at 5000 invoices. Both are best-effort (fail silently).
rules.purpose: Provides super-admin with a revenue health snapshot without blocking the overview on Stripe API failures.
inputs: Stripe paid invoices since Jan 1 (auto-paged, limit 5000)
actions: Sum flat base price amounts per active subscription
Sum paid invoice amounts since Jan 1
failureConditions: Any Stripe API error → returns (0, 0) silently
rules.evidence: if recurring.get('usage_type') == 'metered': continue # overage is variable, not part of MRR ... if recurring.get('interval') == 'year': amount = amount // 12

rules.name: Stripe Read Wrapper — Error-Only Audit (No Audit on Successful Reads)
rules.description: Pure Stripe reads use stripe_read() which audits billing_events ONLY on error. Successful reads produce no audit row, preventing high-volume read polling from flooding the billing_events ledger. Reads are NOT subject to the Connect-attribution block or idempotency keys.
rules.purpose: Keeps billing_events as a write ledger only; prevents noise from frequent read operations like usage summary polls.
actions: On StripeError: INSERT billing_events with status='failed', then re-raise as StripeBillingError
failureConditions: StripeError → StripeBillingError propagated to caller
rules.evidence: def stripe_read(*, op, fn, org_id=None, actor_id=None): # Run a pure Stripe read. Records a billing_events row ONLY on failure.

rules.name: Billing Status UI Labels — German Localization with Soft-Stop Messaging
rules.description: The frontend maps Stripe subscription statuses to German labels: active→Aktiv, trialing→Testphase, past_due→Zahlung überfällig, unpaid→Unbezahlt, canceled→Gekündigt, incomplete→Unvollständig, incomplete_expired→Abgelaufen, paused→Pausiert, legacy→Altvertrag, none→Kein Abo. The over-quota state shows a soft-stop message ('agent stays reachable, overage billed'). The 80% warning is also surfaced in the UI (client-side calculation from used/quota).
rules.purpose: German-language UI for billing state; soft-stop messaging prevents customer panic at over-quota.
actions: Show 80% warning banner if used_percent >= 80 and not over quota
Show past_due payment banner if status=past_due or unpaid
rules.evidence: BILLING_STATUS_LABELS = {active: 'Aktiv', trialing: 'Testphase', past_due: 'Zahlung überfällig', ...} ... {over && Ihr Minutenkontingent ist aufgebraucht. Ihre KI bleibt erreichbar — der Mehrverbrauch wird nach Tarif berechnet.}

rules.name: Subscription Welcome Email — Deduped Per Subscription, Separate from Stripe Receipt
rules.description: When checkout.session.completed is received and the subscription is linked, notify_subscription_activated() sends a HeyKiki-branded welcome email (via Brevo SMTP). This is distinct from Stripe's payment receipt and invoice email. Deduped per subscription_id so a re-delivered webhook cannot double-send. Best-effort: failure does not block subscription linking.
rules.purpose: Confirms to the customer that their KiKi subscription is active and explains cancellation policy (email/phone only).
validations: Dedup key: 'subscription_activated:{subscription_id or org_id}'
actions: Send email via send_email() with HeyKiki branding
systemEffects: Email sent to org contact address
failureConditions: Email failure: logged, notification row status updated to 'failed'; subscription linking unaffected
rules.evidence: dedup_key=f'subscription_activated:{subscription_id or org_id}' ... # OUR welcome email (Brevo) — Stripe owns the receipt + invoice email. Best-effort + deduped per subscription, so it never blocks linking.
